tradeReviews.reason: Sold SPY calls at 1.05 but contract went to 1.185 — exited way too early, left significant profits on the table for a small loss
tradeReviews.lesson: Don't exit too early; let winning trades run to target
tradeReviews.author: Jasper -TP2 Trading

tradeReviews.reason: Entered wrong strike price — bought 237.5c at .54 instead of intended 235c, delta too low at 0.27 so underlying needs to move a lot to profit
tradeReviews.lesson: Double-check strike price before entry; wrong strike kills probability
tradeReviews.author: Mike Y

tradeReviews.reason: Made $3k on NVDA in the morning but couldn't sit on hands, overtraded and gave almost all of it back, ending the day with only $100 total
tradeReviews.lesson: Sit on your hands after a big win; don't overtrade profits away
tradeReviews.author: Jasper -TP2 Trading

tradeReviews.reason: Lost $580 on NVDA and regrets exiting early — should have held the position longer for the full move
tradeReviews.lesson: Trust your thesis and hold for the full move if the setup is still valid
tradeReviews.author: Jasper -TP2 Trading

tradeReviews.reason: Bad entry on TSLA 285 calls — poor entry price forced exit at breakeven, while intended entry of 280 calls at $5 would have doubled to $10
tradeReviews.lesson: Precise entry is critical; a bad entry ruins the entire setup
tradeReviews.author: Heiken-Sushi

tradeReviews.reason: Entered NVDA too early, was stressed the whole time, let it ride past stop loss to give breathing room — it eventually turned around and worked out but risk management was poor
tradeReviews.lesson: Enter at the right time and respect your stop loss; don't rely on luck
tradeReviews.author: OptionPro

tradeReviews.reason: Got a good entry on AMZN at PMH test but got scared when it pulled back, sold prematurely for a small loss instead of holding
tradeReviews.lesson: Trust your analysis; don't let fear make you exit good entries early
tradeReviews.author: demchy19

tradeReviews.reason: Blew through mental stop loss on Tesla, was down $700 — reckless risk management ignoring own stop levels
tradeReviews.lesson: Never blow through your stop losses; respect your risk limits
tradeReviews.author: Stella

tradeReviews.reason: Took TSLA $425 call but exited on a small loss due to inexperience, leaving significant profit on the table
tradeReviews.lesson: Inexperience leads to exiting too early; gain confidence to hold winners
tradeReviews.author: MAN786

tradeReviews.reason: Took 5min ORB break and retest on SPY, sized down due to already being up for the week (good risk management) but left a ton of money on the table after a strong bounce
tradeReviews.lesson: Sizing down protects profits but can cap gains; balance risk management with opportunity
tradeReviews.author: Markellwhite16

tradeReviews.reason: Was too aggressive on the backside of the trade, leading to a $3k-$4k loss — turned a $10k potential day into roughly flat after a series of losses
tradeReviews.lesson: Don't get reckless after a win; one over-aggressive trade can wipe out gains
tradeReviews.author: Andy Pyramidoor (Biz._Decision)

tradeReviews.reason: NVDA trade was a great trade but exited too early just above high of day while it kept pushing — still profitable but could have had 20%+ more
tradeReviews.lesson: Hold longer and wait for signs of weakness before exiting
tradeReviews.author: DWC2016

tradeReviews.reason: Setup was right, entry was good, took first target, respected stop loss (good discipline) but didn't capture the full move and didn't re-enter when it consolidated — process good, result a loss
tradeReviews.lesson: Good process doesn't always mean profit; consider re-entry if thesis is still valid after stop-out
tradeReviews.author: StevenP

tradeReviews.reason: Had a bad entry on AMD — entry price was poor
tradeReviews.lesson: Wait for a proper entry; don't force a trade at a bad price
tradeReviews.author: void

tradeReviews.reason: Entered GOOGL 3 times on good setups but got stopped out on all 3 — market didn't follow through despite solid entries
tradeReviews.lesson: Even good setups can fail; the market ultimately decides
tradeReviews.author: Viper

tradeReviews.reason: Stuck to original thesis through 4 trades — 3 losses on good entries but final trade worked out, ending breakeven. Persistence paid off but took many losses
tradeReviews.lesson: Sticking to a thesis can eventually work, but multiple failed entries erode confidence and capital
tradeReviews.author: Viper

tradeReviews.reason: Had Apple puts that were solid, sold at $4.00/contract but they later went to $9.30 — sold way too early and left massive profits on the table
tradeReviews.pnl: profitable but far less than possible
tradeReviews.lesson: Hold for the bigger move instead of taking small profits early on winners
tradeReviews.author: Stella

tradeReviews.reason: Quick scalp on GOOG — not the best entry but still worked out profitably
tradeReviews.lesson: N/A
tradeReviews.author: Mina

tradeReviews.reason: Took calls above VWAP at 6:50 candle, got stopped out next candle, then the move happened later — bad timing cost -$860 and wiped out weekly gains
tradeReviews.lesson: Entry timing is critical; being too early on a move still loses money
tradeReviews.author: Mk

tradeReviews.reason: Had the perfect QQQ drop play drawn out but got distracted by an NVDA long play, still made money but missed 60% of potential profits
tradeReviews.lesson: Stick to your plan; don't get distracted by other plays
tradeReviews.author: DWC2016

tradeReviews.reason: Was up $2k at $131 on NVDA, held on too long, gave back most profits to end with only $600 — didn't take profits when available
tradeReviews.lesson: Take partial profits along the way; don't get greedy holding for more
tradeReviews.author: Lauren (lakatrades)

tradeReviews.reason: Made bad trades that didn't fit the break-and-retest strategy, now holding NVDA long hoping for a bounce back — no plan, just hoping
tradeReviews.lesson: Only trade your strategy; holding and hoping is not a plan
tradeReviews.author: Boat Hobo

tradeReviews.reason: Lost $450 on NQ — tried to trade on the subway resulting in a bad entry in a poor trading environment
tradeReviews.lesson: Don't trade in distracting environments; wait until you can focus
tradeReviews.author: TG

tradeReviews.reason: QQQ hit PDL target but got stopped out before reaching it — followed rules and didn't crash out but left $240+ extra profit on the table
tradeReviews.lesson: Following your rules is more important than maximizing every trade
tradeReviews.author: DWC2016

tradeReviews.reason: Took QQQ, made only $185 after getting stopped out on last contract, should have been up $450-$500 but followed rules — good discipline despite leaving money on table
tradeReviews.lesson: Following rules consistently matters more than any single day's result
tradeReviews.author: DWC2016

tradeReviews.reason: Good trade, followed the plan, but couldn't scale properly with only 2 contracts — limited profit potential but executed discipline well
tradeReviews.lesson: Good risk management with position sizing that fits account size
tradeReviews.author: DWC2016

tradeReviews.reason: Traded 2 QQQ puts, took TP of $189, then got back in out of greed and gave back profits, ending with only $112 — couldn't walk away after hitting target
tradeReviews.lesson: Walk away after hitting your target; greed to make more costs you
tradeReviews.author: TCTrader1
